title: phemeranthus parvulus
intro: Phemeranthus parvulus, also known as the sandmarigold or desert marigold, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a perennial herb that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of P. parvulus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiviral activity, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, P. parvulus is often used as a dried flower or root extract, and can be consumed as a tea, capsule, or topical cream to harness its medicinal properties.
